_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A lazy approach would add CSS to enlarge the font for grade 1 quiz answers.

A minimal JavaScript change could toggle a class on the card or play element when the collection ID is grade-1-quiz. Alternatively, a body-level class might work. Focus mode is the intended play view.

Grade 1 quiz answers will use clamped font sizes up to 4.5rem in focus mode and 3.5rem otherwise. Letter spacing will be increased for readability.

The card element must be added to the elements object. Offline HTML exports should also receive the larger font.

Offline HTML lacks the collection ID needed to identify grade-1-quiz mode. Adding `collection_id` to the offline payload would allow applying the larger answer class during offline rendering.

The offline payload will carry a `large_answer` boolean flag. Offline JavaScript will apply the larger answer class to the card or answer element when this flag is present. The offline CSS also needs to include the enlarged font styles.

The exact font size for grade 1 quiz answers is now set to clamp(2.5rem, 10vw, 4.5rem). This makes the answer larger than the focus-mode prompt so children can easily read the short words.
</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Scoping a larger answer font to the grade 1 quiz via a card class and minimal JS.


_**Agent (auto-low)**_

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Grade 1 quiz answers now use a much larger font — roughly 2.75rem–5rem instead of 1.25rem–1.75rem — so short words like `CAT` or `DOG` are easy for a first grader to read when revealed.

Changes:
- **`custom.css`** — `.randomizer-card--grade-1 .randomizer-card__answer` bumps size and letter-spacing
- **`randomizer.js`** — applies that class when playing `grade-1-quiz`, including in saved offline HTML

Other quiz decks keep the original answer size. Open `/randomizer/?set=grade-1-quiz`, shuffle a question, and tap **Show answer** to check it.
